51.reason: Error: Logical condition is always true. Should be (left!='*' && left!='/') instead of (left!='*'||left!='/')

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Logical condition is always true. Should be (left!='*' && left!='/') instead of (left!='*'||left!='/')
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Logical condition is always true. Should be (left!='*' && left!='/') instead of (left!='*'||left!='/')
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: logical condition is always true. Intended condition should be ((left=='+'||left=='-') && (right=='*'||right=='/')). This causes incorrect operator precedence handling, leading to wrong results and eventually division by zero.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Condition always true, causing incorrect precedence handling and eventual division by zero]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Logical Error: The condition `left!='*'||left!='/'` is always true because `left` cannot be both '*' and '/' simultaneously. This means `judge` is set to 1 whenever `right` is '*' or '/', regardless of `left`. This breaks the precedence logic. It should be `left!='*' && left!='/'` (or equivalently `(left=='+' || left=='-')`).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logic Error: The condition `left!='*'||left!='/'` is always true because `left` cannot be both '*' and '/' simultaneously. This means `judge` is set to 1 whenever `right` is '*' or '/', regardless of `left`. The correct logic to check if `left` has lower precedence than `right` (i.e., `left` is + or - and `right` is * or /) should be `(left=='+' || left=='-') && (right=='*' || right=='/')`. The current logic fails to correctly handle operator precedence, leading to incorrect order of operations.
